title: Chaetanthera revoluta
taxonomy: Chaetanthera revoluta is classified as a flowering plant, specifically a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chaetanthera, which consists of several species of plants native to South America. The genus Chaetanthera is further divided into several species, with Chaetanthera revoluta being one of the most well-known. Chaetanthera revoluta is a perennial plant that grows in various environments, from grasslands to mountainous regions. It is closely related to other members of the Asteraceae family, which includes daisies, sunflowers, and many other common wildflowers.

intro: Chaetanthera revoluta, also known as the Chilean yellow chamomile, is a perennial herb native to the Andes Mountains in South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its soothing and calming properties. The bioactive constituents of Chaetanthera revoluta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
